description: Catalytic domain (residues 403-621) of S. cerevisiae Ulp1 (UniProt Q02724), the SUMO/Smt3-specific cysteine protease that cleaves after the SUMO C-terminal di-glycine to release a native N-terminus; cognate protease of the SUMO fusion tag.

references.title: Ulp1-SUMO crystal structure and genetic analysis reveal conserved interactions and a regulatory element essential for cell growth in yeast

references.authors: Malakhov MP, Mattern MR, Malakhova OA, Drinker M, Weeks SD, Butt TR
references.title: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins
references.journal: Journal of Structural and Functional Genomics (2004)

references.title: The Ulp1 SUMO isopeptidase: distinct domains required for viability, nuclear envelope localization, and substrate specificity

references.authors: Lau YK, Baytshtok V, Howard TA, Fiala BM, Johnson JM, Carter LP, Baker D, Lima CD, Bahl CD
references.title: Discovery and engineering of enhanced SUMO protease enzymes

references.authors: Zhang F, Zheng H, Xian Y, Song H, Wang S, Yun Y, Yi L, Zhang G
references.title: Profiling Substrate Specificity of the SUMO Protease Ulp1 by the YESS-PSSC System to Advance the Conserved Mechanism for Substrate Cleavage

references.authors: Linova MY, Risør MW, Jørgensen SE, Mansour Z, Kaya J, Sigurdarson JJ, Enghild JJ, Karring H
references.title: A novel approach for production of an active N-terminally truncated Ulp1 (SUMO protease 1) catalytic domain from Escherichia coli inclusion bodies

references.authors: Marblestone JG, Edavettal SC, Lim Y, Lim P, Zuo X, Butt TR
references.title: Comparison of SUMO fusion technology with traditional gene fusion systems: enhanced expression and solubility with SUMO

label: Ulp1
note: Catalytic (UD/Ulp) domain of Saccharomyces cerevisiae Ulp1, residues 403-621 of the 621-aa UniProt Q02724 (ULP1_YEAST). SUMO/Smt3-specific cysteine protease (clan CE, SENP/Ulp family) that cleaves after the SUMO C-terminal Gly-Gly to release a passenger with a native (scarless) N-terminus. This minimal catalytic fragment is the canonical 403-621 crystallography/expression construct (PDB 1EUV) and is sufficient for the essential SUMO-deconjugation function in vivo.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:Q02724 (ULP1_YEAST, Saccharomyces cerevisiae; canonical sequence SV=1, 621 aa), residues 403-621, 100% identity over 219 aa

functional_claims.label: Ulp1 is a cysteine protease that uses a Cys-His-Asp catalytic triad (Cys580-His514-Asp531) and recognizes the folded 3D structure of SUMO rather than a linear sequence motif

value.recognition: fold-dependent recognition of the globular SUMO/Smt3 domain
value.cleavage: after the SUMO C-terminal Gly-Gly, releasing a native N-terminus

source.quote: Ulp1-SUMO crystal structure and genetic analysis reveal conserved interactions and a regulatory element essential for cell growth in yeast

functional_claims.label: Ulp1 is the cognate protease that removes an N-terminal SUMO fusion tag, cleaving at the SUMO C-terminal Gly-Gly to release the passenger with a native (scarless) N-terminus

value.cleavage_site: immediately after the SUMO C-terminal Gly-Gly
value.recognition: tertiary structure of SUMO, not a linear motif
value.product: passenger protein with a native N-terminus

source.quote: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins

functional_claims.label: Cleavage tolerates most residues at the P1' (+1) position with the notable exception of proline; P1/P2 of the substrate (the SUMO Gly-Gly) shows some flexibility but cleavage fidelity depends on the P1' residue

value.p1_p2: some flexibility at the SUMO C-terminus (Ala/Ser/Cys tolerated; strict Gly-Gly not absolutely required)
value.consequence: an N-terminal proline on the desired product blocks scarless release

source.quote: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins

functional_claims.label: Native to Saccharomyces cerevisiae (ULP1 gene product); the recombinant 403-621 catalytic domain is deployed mainly as a purified protease for SUMO-fusion tag removal, with production primarily in Escherichia coli

value.use_context: in vitro tag removal of SUMO-fusion proteins

source.quote: The Ulp1 SUMO isopeptidase: distinct domains required for viability, nuclear envelope localization, and substrate specificity

functional_claims.label: The 403-621 fragment is the minimal catalytic (UD/Ulp) domain; in vivo the catalytic domain alone (from ~aa 417) is sufficient for the essential SUMO-deconjugation function, while the N-terminal region (aa 1-346) governs nuclear-envelope localization and substrate specificity

value.minimal_essential: catalytic domain alone (from ~aa 417) sufficient for viability
value.n_terminal_domain: aa 1-346 confers NPC/nuclear-envelope localization and shapes substrate specificity
value.fold: alpha/beta protease fold (reported as ~7 alpha + 7 beta secondary-structure elements)

source.quote: The Ulp1 SUMO isopeptidase: distinct domains required for viability, nuclear envelope localization, and substrate specificity

functional_claims.label: Used as a high-activity protease for scarless removal of N-terminal SUMO solubility/expression tags; reported to act under a broad range of buffer and temperature conditions

value.application: scarless removal of SUMO-fusion tags to expose a native N-terminus
value.conditions: active across a broad buffer/temperature range (per source)
value.relative_activity: reported kcat substantially higher than AcTEV protease (quantitative value not verified from primary full text)

source.quote: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins

functional_claims.label: The Ulp1(403-621) catalytic fragment is toxic to E. coli host cells, complicating its recombinant production; an N-terminally truncated 416-621 form (the first 13 aa of the fragment are dispensable for activity) can be produced from inclusion bodies and refolded as an alternative

value.issue: host-cell toxicity of soluble Ulp1(403-621) during E. coli expression
value.workaround: Ulp1(416-621) produced as inclusion bodies and refolded; the N-terminal 13 aa of the 403-621 fragment are not required for catalytic activity
value.approx_yield_workaround: ~80 mg/L after refolding (per source; not verified from primary full text)

source.quote: A novel approach for production of an active N-terminally truncated Ulp1 (SUMO protease 1) catalytic domain from Escherichia coli inclusion bodies

functional_claims.label: Engineered solubility-enhanced variants (Ulp1_R1-R4, computationally redesigned, up to ~12x improved solubility with retained WT catalytic activity) and a divergent Chaetomium thermophilum SUMO protease (~28% identity, ~4x higher Vmax; PDB 6DG4) have been developed from / alongside the Ulp1 403-621 wild-type construct

value.engineered_property: up to ~12x improved solubility, WT-level catalytic activity (computational solubility redesign)
value.orthologous_enzyme: Chaetomium thermophilum SUMO protease (~28% identity, ~4x higher Vmax, PDB 6DG4)
value.relation_to_this_part: this part is the wild-type S. cerevisiae 403-621 catalytic domain; the listed variants are derivatives/orthologs, not this exact sequence

source.quote: Discovery and engineering of enhanced SUMO protease enzymes
